top_signals.title: LA’s $1.2 Billion ‘Graffiti Towers’ Cleared to Exit Bankruptcy
top_signals.summary: The bankrupt $1.2 billion Oceanwide Plaza project in Los Angeles, dubbed the Graffiti Towers after taggers spray-painted the abandoned complex, has been cleared for sale and possible completion more than seven years after the original Chinese developer ran out of money.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Sinaloa cartel founder handed $15bn forfeiture order and life sentence
top_signals.summary: ‘El Mayo’, who co-founded group alongside ‘El Chapo’, was sentenced to life in prison

top_signals.title: Burnham surprises Labour MPs by choosing Healey as new chancellor
top_signals.summary: UK prime minister’s choice of former defence minister surprises allies

top_signals.title: Wealthy Brits fear Burnham tax consequences
top_signals.summary: Wealthy Brits are bracing for a major shake-up of the tax system, as the UK’s new prime minister looks for ways to fund fresh spending commitments. Prior to taking office, Andy Burnham had already hinted at plans over how the country should be taxed. The Makerfield MP has so far refused to rule out a [...]
